docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Modeling, AI, and Optimization for Engineering Design

We develop methods for simulation-based design optimization, dimensionality reduction, surrogate modeling, and machine learning for complex engineering systems, with applications in marine, aerospace, and multidisciplinary vehicle design.

Research People Projects Software

docs.title: About
docs.text:
The MAO Research Group works on advanced computational methods for engineering design, with a focus on:

  • simulation-based design optimization
  • dimensionality reduction for shape optimization
  • physics-informed and multi-fidelity surrogate models
  • machine learning for marine and aerospace applications
  • reproducible computational workflows and open scientific software

docs.title: Research Areas
docs.text:
Dimensionality Reduction

Reduced-order representations of high-dimensional design spaces, including PME and its physics-informed variants.

Simulation-Based Design Optimization

Optimization frameworks for expensive engineering simulations, including multi-objective and many-fidelity settings.

Scientific Machine Learning

Integration of machine learning with physical models for design exploration, prediction, and interpretability.

Marine and Aerospace Applications

Applications to ships, propellers, underwater vehicles, airfoils, and advanced transportation systems.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This page collects datasets used for dimensionality reduction, optimization benchmarks, and reproducible computational studies.

docs.title: Glider benchmark dataset
docs.text:
Dataset for dimensionality reduction and optimization studies on a bio-inspired autonomous underwater glider.

Includes

  • geometry representation
  • design variables
  • physical outputs
  • metadata and preprocessing information

docs.title: Propeller dataset
docs.text:
Dataset for marine propeller shape analysis and optimization.

Includes

  • parametric geometry information
  • hydrodynamic quantities
  • benchmark metadata

docs.title: Airfoil dataset
docs.text:
Dataset for aerodynamic shape optimization and dimensionality reduction studies.

Includes

  • parameterized geometries
  • pressure distributions
  • integrated aerodynamic coefficients

docs.title: Reproducibility Policy
docs.text:
For each dataset, documentation should specify:

  • origin
  • variable definitions
  • preprocessing steps
  • file formats
  • sharing constraints
  • reproducibility notes

docs.title: Join the Group
docs.text:
We welcome interest from students, visiting researchers, postdoctoral researchers, and collaborators working on:

  • optimization
  • machine learning for engineering
  • scientific computing
  • marine and aerospace design
  • reduced-order modeling

docs.title: Potential Topics
docs.text:
Examples of thesis or research topics include:

  • dimensionality reduction for engineering design
  • multi-fidelity surrogate modeling
  • explainable optimization workflows
  • bio-inspired underwater vehicle design
  • physics-informed machine learning

docs.title: How to Apply
docs.text:
Please send:

  • a short motivation message
  • CV
  • research interests
  • optional transcript or portfolio of relevant work

to the contact listed on the Contact page.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Our research focuses on computational methods for engineering design under complexity, uncertainty, and high computational cost.

We are particularly interested in methods that improve:

  • design-space exploration
  • optimization efficiency
  • physical interpretability
  • reproducibility of computational results

docs.title: Design-space dimensionality reduction
docs.text:
We develop methods to reduce the dimensionality of high-dimensional parametric design spaces while preserving relevant geometric and physical information.

Representative topics include:

  • principal component analysis and modal parameterization
  • parametric model embedding (PME)
  • physics-informed and physics-driven dimensionality reduction
  • backmapping and parametric consistency

docs.title: Simulation-based design optimization
docs.text:
We investigate optimization strategies for expensive numerical simulations, including:

  • single- and multi-objective optimization
  • surrogate-based optimization
  • adaptive sampling
  • many-fidelity and multi-fidelity strategies
  • robust and uncertainty-aware optimization

docs.title: Scientific machine learning
docs.text:
We study how machine learning can support engineering design through:

  • surrogate modeling
  • reduced-order modeling
  • data-driven representation learning
  • explainability for design decisions
  • hybrid physics-data-driven approaches

docs.title: Application domains
docs.text:
Typical application domains include:

  • ship hull design
  • marine propellers
  • autonomous underwater vehicles
  • bio-inspired marine systems
  • airfoils and aerodynamic configurations
  • multidisciplinary vehicle design

docs.title: Philosophy
docs.text:
Our methodological approach emphasizes:

  • physical consistency
  • computational efficiency
  • interpretability
  • reproducibility
  • open and reusable research software

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The group develops research software to support reproducible engineering design, dimensionality reduction, and simulation-based optimization workflows.

docs.title: PME-toolkit
docs.text:
Toolkit for parametric model embedding and related methods for design-space dimensionality reduction in engineering shape optimization.

Main features

  • reduced-order representation of design spaces
  • analytical backmapping
  • support for physics-informed variants
  • MATLAB and Python-oriented development

Resources

  • Repository: link here
  • Documentation: link here
  • Citation: link here

docs.title: SAMOA
docs.text:
Adaptive surrogate-based framework for multi-fidelity optimization and analysis.

Main features

  • surrogate-assisted optimization
  • adaptive sampling
  • uncertainty-aware workflows

Resources

  • Repository: link here

docs.title: Software Principles
docs.text:
Our software development follows these principles:

  • scientific transparency
  • reproducibility
  • versioned releases
  • maintainable documentation
  • clear separation between stable and experimental features
